message:
fix(table): use saturating arithmetic to prevent overflow in size calculations

The MeasurableWidget implementation for Table now uses saturating
arithmetic throughout the width and height calculations:

- Separator width uses saturating_mul instead of direct multiplication
- Column width summation uses fold with saturating_add instead of sum()
- Row height summation uses fold with saturating_add instead of sum()

This prevents panic from arithmetic overflow when tables have many
columns/rows or very wide content, which could previously occur in
edge cases with extreme dimensions. The saturating behavior clamps
to u16::MAX instead of panicking, providing graceful degradation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
